---
title: "Sidecar Inventory"
sidebar: scenarios
---

A sidecar inventory lets a data archive improve metadata without rewriting raw files.

## Proposal Sketch

```yaml
format: fas_inventory
schema_version: 1
resource_id: versailles_test_inventory
creation_info:
  agency_id: EXAMPLE
  author: Field Team
  version: "1"
resources:
  interrogator_1:
    type: Interrogator
    manufacturer: Example Instruments
    model: DAS-1000
  cable_1:
    type: Cable
    name: Garden sensing cable
    fiber_count: 4
networks:
  - code: XX
    name: Example network
    fiber_arrays:
      - code: GARDEN
        name: Garden fiber array
        acquisitions:
          - code: RAW
            location_code: ""
            data_category: DAS
            data_type: strain_rate
            data_units: "1/s"
            interrogator: interrogator_1
            acquisition_sample_rate: 250.0
            gauge_length: 10.0
            spatial_sampling_interval: 1.0
```

The inventory does not need to be complete on day one. Unknown intervals can still carry an `optical_length`, and metadata can be refined as field notes are digitized.

## What This Enables

- Raw data files stay unchanged.
- Metadata can be versioned independently.
- Multiple data products can point to the same inventory context.
- Partial metadata is still useful because unknown sections are explicit.
